title: ～がゆえの Noun
titleEn: Expresses the reason or cause for something; 'because of', 'due to'.
structure: Noun / Verb-dictionary form + がゆえの + Noun
structureEn: Noun / Verb-dictionary form + がゆえの + Noun
explanation: Expresses the reason or cause for something; 'because of', 'due to'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～がゆえの Noun pattern is used to describe a noun that exists or occurs due to some cause. It is often used in more literary or formal contexts.

title: ～から Noun に 至る まで
titleEn: Expressing range from one thing to another; 'from...to', 'ranging from...to'.
structure: Noun (start) + から + Noun (end) + に至るまで
structureEn: Noun (start) + から + Noun (end) + に至るまで
explanation: Expressing range from one thing to another; 'from...to', 'ranging from...to'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～から〜に至るまで grammar point emphasizes a range or scope extending from one extreme to another. It can be translated as 'from...to...' or 'ranging from...to...' in English. It can apply to people, places, times, objects, and so on.

title: ～ごとく
titleEn: To seem like; as if; as though.
structure: Verb-て + ごとく / Noun + の + ごとく
structureEn: Verb-て + ごとく / Noun + の + ごとく
explanation: To seem like; as if; as though. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ごとく grammar point is used to express similarity or resemblance. It is quite formal/literary, meaning 'as if,' 'as though,' or 'like.' It can be used with verbs and nouns.

title: ～こととて
titleEn: Used to express the reason or premise for a decision or judgment; 'since', 'because'.
structure: Verb (plain form) + こととて / Noun + の + こととて / (な-Adjective + な) + こととて
structureEn: Verb (plain form) + こととて / Noun + の + こととて / (な-Adjective + な) + こととて
explanation: Used to express the reason or premise for a decision or judgment; 'since', 'because'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～こととて grammar point expresses a reason or premise in a slightly formal or literary way. It can often be interpreted as 'since' or 'because'.

title: ～ずにすんだ
titleEn: Expresses the idea of 'was able to do without'; 'managed to avoid'.
structure: Verb-ず form + に + すんだ
structureEn: Verb-ず form + に + すんだ
explanation: Expresses the idea of 'was able to do without'; 'managed to avoid'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ずにすんだ grammar point indicates that one was able to avoid doing something or managed to get by without doing it. It translates as 'managed to avoid ~' or 'got by without ~.'

title: ～だろうとなかろうと
titleEn: Expresses 'regardless of whether it is or isn't'.
structure: Noun / i-Adjective / na-Adjective / Verb + だろうとなかろうと
structureEn: Noun / i-Adjective / na-Adjective / Verb + だろうとなかろうと
explanation: Expresses 'regardless of whether it is or isn't'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～だろうとなかろうと grammar point is used to express the idea of 'regardless of whether it is or isn't.' It translates roughly as 'whether it is ~ or not' in English. This construction emphasizes that the speaker’s stance or action does not change, regardless of the two possible conditions.

title: ～つもりだ
titleEn: Expresses the speaker's intention or plan.
explanation: Expresses the speaker's intention or plan. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～つもりだ grammar point is used to express the speaker's intention or plan to do something. It translates as 'intend to,' 'plan to,' or 'think that (I will).' It's often used with the verb in its dictionary form.

title: ～つもりで
titleEn: Expresses the speaker's intention or plan; 'with the intention of', 'planning to'.
explanation: Expresses the speaker's intention or plan; 'with the intention of', 'planning to'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～つもりで grammar point is used to express the speaker's intention or mindset when doing something. It can often be translated as 'with the intention of' or 'with the mindset that.'

title: ～ではすまない
titleEn: Expresses that just doing something is not enough or will not suffice.
structure: Verb-dictionary form + ではすまない / い-Adjective + ではすまない / な-Adjective + ではすまない / Noun + ではすまない
structureEn: Verb-dictionary form + ではすまない / い-Adjective + ではすまない / な-Adjective + ではすまない / Noun + ではすまない
explanation: Expresses that just doing something is not enough or will not suffice. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point ～ではすまない is used when the speaker strongly feels that an action or situation alone is not sufficient or acceptable, implying that more serious measures are needed or that the consequences are more severe.

title: ～とあって
titleEn: Expresses a special situation or event due to certain circumstances.
structure: Noun + とあって / (Sometimes Verb + とあって, depending on context)
structureEn: Noun + とあって / (Sometimes Verb + とあって, depending on context)
explanation: Expresses a special situation or event due to certain circumstances. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～とあって grammar point is used to indicate that, due to a special or noteworthy circumstance, a particular situation has occurred. It often translates as 'because (it is)...' or 'given that...' in English, emphasizing the uniqueness of the cause.
